ddls:
CREATE TABLE "funds" (
  "fund_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each fund */,
  "fund_name" TEXT /* Name of the mutual fund */,
  "inception_date" TEXT /* Date the fund was established */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of fund (e.g., Small Cap, Retail Shares) */,
  "asset_class" TEXT /* The asset class of the fund (e.g., equity, fixed income) */,
  "investment_strategy" TEXT /* The investment strategy of the fund (e.g., growth, value, index) */,
  "fund_manager" TEXT /* The name of the fund manager */,
  "management_fee" REAL /* The annual management fee as a percentage */,
  "expense_ratio" REAL /* The total annual expense ratio as a percentage */,
  "minimum_investment" REAL /* The minimum investment required to enter the fund */,
  "performance_fee" REAL /* The performance fee as a percentage, if applicable */,
  "currency" TEXT /* The currency in which the fund is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("fund_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "returns" (
  "return_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each return rate */,
  "fund_id" INTEGER /* ID of the fund the return rate belongs to */,
  "return_type" TEXT /* Type of return (e.g., before taxes, after taxes on distributions, after taxes on distributions and sale of fund shares) */,
  "time_period" TEXT /* Time period for the return rate (e.g., 1 year, 5 years, 10 years, since inception) */,
  "return_rate" REAL /* Return rate as a percentage */,
  "as_of_date" TEXT /* The date when the return rate was last updated */,
  "benchmark" TEXT /* The benchmark index used for comparison (e.g., S&P 500) */,
  "benchmark_return_rate" REAL /* The return rate of the benchmark for the same time period */,
  "alpha" REAL /* The alpha value, which measures the fund's performance relative to its benchmark */,
  "beta" REAL /* The beta value, which measures the fund's sensitivity to market movements */,
  "sharpe_ratio" REAL /* The Sharpe ratio, which measures the risk-adjusted return */,
  "sortino_ratio" REAL /* The Sortino ratio, which measures the risk-adjusted return focusing on downside risk */,
  "information_ratio" REAL /* The information ratio, which measures the fund's excess return per unit of tracking error */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("return_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_returns_fund_id FOREIGN KEY ("fund_id") REFERENCES funds ("fund_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., financial analyst, portfolio manager, admin) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* The phone number of the user */,
  "address" TEXT /* The address of the user */,
  "date_joined" TEXT /* The date when the user joined the system */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* The date of the user's last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* The status of the user (e.g., active, suspended) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "fund_id" INTEGER /* ID of the fund being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the fund */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the fund was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* The IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* The user agent string of the device used for access */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* The duration of the access in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_fund_id FOREIGN KEY ("fund_id") REFERENCES funds ("fund_id")
)
